meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Design with AI to Solve Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Overcome the frustration of debugging code. Learn how to design with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code in this comprehensive guide for student developers.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a failure; it's a diagnostic skill. By shifting your workflow to 'AI-assisted design,' you treat code as a secondary output of logic. Use Antigravity to architect your structure and Claude Code to iterate through errors, turning every bug into a structured learning opportunity rather than a blocking wall.
content.intro:
You stare at the screen. The terminal is a graveyard of red text, and the logic that felt so clear in your head has collapsed into a mountain of syntax errors. It is the defining moment for every student developer: the realization that writing code is easy, but making it work is the true craft. The weight of 'can't debug code' feels like a ceiling on your potential.

But the game has changed. You are no longer a lone programmer fighting the compiler; you are an architect overseeing an AI-powered engine. By integrating design-first principles with tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform those frustrating errors into clear, actionable architectural feedback. It is time to stop guessing and start building with intent.

steps.title: Architect the Logic Flow
steps.description: Before touching code, define your data structures. This prevents the 'spaghetti code' that makes debugging impossible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Software Architect. I am building a [Project Name]. Define the core data schema and the functional flow for the primary feature. Keep it modular.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --prompt 'Create a README and folder structure based on the architectural plan generated by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Initialize with Type Safety
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold boilerplate code with strict typing to catch errors before they even reach the runtime.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a type-safe boilerplate for my [Project Name] using TypeScript. Ensure all interfaces are clearly defined and documented.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --apply 'Generate the core file structure and type definitions based on the provided architecture.'

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: When a bug occurs, use Claude Code to perform a root cause analysis rather than just pasting error messages.
steps.antigravity_prompt: My code is failing at [Function Name]. Analyze the logic flow and identify if the issue is a race condition or a type mismatch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --chat 'Analyze the recent crash in [File Name]. Explain the root cause and propose a fix that follows SOLID principles.'

steps.title: Implement Unit Tests
steps.description: Stop debugging manually. Write tests that prove your code works, allowing the AI to verify your logic automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of Jest tests for [Module Name] that covers edge cases and potential null values.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --apply 'Generate a comprehensive test suite for the current module and run it in the terminal.'

steps.title: Refactor for Readability
steps.description: Clean code is easier to debug. Use AI to refactor complex blocks into readable, maintainable functions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this block of code. Refactor it for maximum readability and performance, explaining the changes made.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --apply 'Refactor the logic in [File Name] to eliminate technical debt and improve maintainability.'

pro_tips: Always ask the AI to explain 'why' a fix works, not just provide the code. This is how you gain the engineering intuition to solve future bugs yourself.
Treat your error logs as data inputs for your AI agent; provide the full stack trace rather than just the last line.
Use version control (Git) to commit before every major AI-suggested refactor; it allows you to roll back if the AI introduces a 'hallucinated' bug.
Ask your AI to generate a 'debugging checklist' specific to your stack to use whenever you hit a wall.
Focus on building small, atomic features. Large codebases are harder to debug; modularity is your best defense.
content.next_steps: Once you have completed this project, document your debugging journey in a blog post or portfolio entry to showcase your problem-solving process to potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to dismantle any bug that stands in your way. Stop fearing the terminal and start mastering the flow of information. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
